file_name: SLOVAKWIRELESSFINANCECOBV_03_28_2001-EX-4.(B)(II).3-Maintenance and support contract for SICAP(R) modules.txt

perturbation.original_text: EuroTel defines up to 5 system administrators from their own staff. They must be trained for the following modules: o SICAP(R)VCA o SICAP(R)PPB o SICAP(R)OTA Only these system administrators have the permission to use the HotLine and the "GNATS tool".
perturbation.changed_text: EuroTel defines up to 5 system administrators from their own staff. They must be trained for the following modules: o SICAP(R)VCA o SICAP(R)PPB o SICAP(R)OTA Only these trained employees have the permission to use the HotLine and the "GNATS tool".
perturbation.explanation: The term 'system administrators' is replaced with 'trained employees' in this section. This introduces uncertainty and contradicts the defined role and specific training requirements for system administrators, potentially allowing other trained employees without the specific administrator training to access sensitive systems. This creates confusion regarding who is authorized to use the HotLine and the 'GNATS tool'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The HotLine can be reached as follows: Phone: +41 878 807 387 (24 hours, 7 days a week) Fax: +41 41 360 10 29 Trouble Ticket: http:www.swissgsm.ch/gnats/SKEUROTEL (WEB based "GNATS problem report system", used by ISC, Lucerne) EuroTel defines up to 5 system administrators from their own staff. They must be trained for the following modules: o SICAP(R)VCA o SICAP(R)PPB o SICAP(R)OTA Only these trained employees have the permission to use the HotLine and the "GNATS tool".
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defines who has access to the HotLine and 'GNATS tool' based on their role as 'system administrators' and their specific training. The changed text broadens this access to 'trained employees,' removing the specificity of the system administrator role and its associated training. This directly contradicts the intention of limiting access to individuals with specific qualifications, as outlined in both the original and contradicted text snippets. The contradicted text explicitly states the 'system administrators' and their training, but the changed text would allow any 'trained employees' to have access which is a clear contraction. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as the change introduces uncertainty and contradicts the defined role and specific training requirements for system administrators, potentially allowing other trained employees without the specific administrator training to access sensitive systems.

perturbation.original_text: Emergency Priority Incident Critical, complete HA-System failure or a major impact on prepaid functionality. Priority Incident Incident causing a major impact on the SICAP(R) System's functionality. Conditions that prevent EuroTel normal business operations from being carried out in timely manner. o Medium Priority Incident Incident causing a partial or minor impact on the System's functionality o Low Priority Incident Incident having a low impact on the System's functionality such as screen or report layout changes.
perturbation.changed_text: Emergency Priority Incident Critical, complete HA-System failure or a major impact on prepaid functionality. Priority Situation Incident causing a major impact on the SICAP(R) System's functionality. Conditions that prevent EuroTel normal business operations from being carried out in timely manner. o Medium Priority Incident Incident causing a partial or minor impact on the System's functionality o Low Priority Incident Incident having a low impact on the System's functionality such as screen or report layout changes.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Priority Incident' is replaced with 'Priority Situation'. This undermines the defined hierarchy of incident severity. If 'Priority Incident' and 'Priority Situation' are not defined as the same, the response times detailed in section 3.1 could be contested. 'Priority Incident' is specifically what section 3.1 refers to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Response Time during the HotLine availability hours shall be the following: -------------------------------------------------------------------------- Priority of reques Availability Response Time -------------------------------------------------------------------------- emergency 24 hours, 7 days a week 4 hours -------------------------------------------------------------------------- high during working hours 4 hours -------------------------------------------------------------------------- medium during working hours next working day -------------------------------------------------------------------------- low during working hours day after next working day -------------------------------------------------------------------------- It is up to EuroTel to decide which priority occurs. In case of an obvious misuse, Sicap Ltd has the right to charge the costs. Sicap Ltd will perform every possibility to make the system running as soon as possible. Staff of EuroTel must be on-site on request of Sicap Ltd.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Priority Incident' which is used in the contradicted text to specify response times. The changed text replaces 'Priority Incident' with 'Priority Situation'. Since 'Priority Situation' is not defined and the contradicted text specifically refers to 'Priority Incident', the response times are no longer clearly applicable, creating a contradiction regarding which response times apply to which situations. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' which states that using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: In case of a substantial breach of this maintenance and support contract by either party, which is not remedied within thirty days from the other party's notice thereof, the other party shall have the right to terminate this maintenance and support contract with immediate effect without juridical procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: In case of a material breach of this maintenance and support agreement by either party, which is not remedied within sixty days from the other party's notice thereof, the other party shall have the right to terminate this maintenance and support contract with immediate effect without juridical procedures.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'substantial breach' is replaced with 'material breach', and 'contract' changed to 'agreement', while the 'thirty days' becomes 'sixty days' to remedy, potentially creating ambiguity. In legal contexts, 'substantial' and 'material' can have different interpretations, especially in relation to the threshold required for termination. Furthermore, agreement and contract may be interpreted differently by law. The combination of these changes, alongside the altered timeframe, introduces uncertainty about the conditions under which the contract can be terminated. The definition states the original 30 days and substantial breach, but now in the article, 7.5, it is being referred to a different period and a different type of breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term and termination This contract replaces in full the Annex 7 "Maintenance and Support for SICAP(R) modules PPB, VCA and OTA and Sun Hardware" which is an integral part of the original Swisscom PURCHASE & LICENSE CONTRACT, made effective on 06 April 1998. It shall be effective on 06 October 2000 and shall have a duration of two years. The prices remain valid for the first 12 months. After this period, Sicap Ltd reserves the right to adjust the prices annually. The offer must be submitted three (3) months in advance and EuroTel may decide within one (1) month to prolong or terminate this maintenance and support contract. If EuroTel doesn't react in writing, the new offer shall be considered as accepted. After the initial period of two years, the maintenance and support contract shall be automatically renewed for a period of one year on each renewal date, unless one of the parties terminates the maintenance and support contract through written notification to the other party in the form of a registered letter with proof of receipt, at least six (6) weeks prior to the renewal date. There shall be no reimbursement of any paid fee. In case of a material breach of this maintenance and support agreement by either party, which is not remedied within sixty days from the other party's notice thereof, the other party shall have the right to terminate this maintenance and support contract with immediate effect without juridical procedures. The termination or expiration of the maintenance and support contract for any reason whatever shall be without prejudice to any other right or obligation of any party hereto in respect of this maintenance and support contract which have arisen prior to such termination.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'substantial breach' to 'material breach' and 'thirty days' to 'sixty days' to remedy, directly contradicts the initial understanding of breach and the timeframe for resolution elsewhere in the contradicted text. This creates a direct conflict, as the conditions for termination are now altered, leading to uncertainty about which terms should be enforced. The introduction of 'agreement' instead of 'contract' also adds to the ambiguity, fitting the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition. The contradicted section explicitly mentions these terms, which are now rendered inconsistent, causing uncertainty and potential legal disputes. Furthermore, the changed text is now in the same location as the contradicted text and it strongly contradicts what was previously stated.
